title: Dry Bulk Shipping - July 3, 2018
summary: Dry bulk rates continue to hover in recent ranges - Capesize rates reached 19,000/d, breaking out of the Atlantic Capesize fixing strong - The 4-week moving average on Atlantic basin Capesize fixtures saw a Seasonality about to kick in - the second half of the year has historically experienced stronger rates, on Futures curve reflects expectations for small increase in rates - The Freight Futures curve prices in a small Scrapping remains at very low levels - YTD, less than 1% of the global dry bulk fleet as been demolished, Short term outlook neutral - With expectations for high iron ore exports in the second half of the year (up Long term outlook neutral - Iron ore exports remain the most predictable demand driver for dry bulk,
